sections.paragraph: m. Isa 53:2, and יֹונֶקֶת f. properly sucking, figuratively a sucker of a tree, as if it sucked nourishment from a mother. Job 8:16 14:7 15:30 Eze 17:22 Hos 14:7. By a similar figure applied from animals to plants, a sucker is called in Greek μόσχος, and pullulare is used of plants.
